intro: Elymus sclerophyllus, also known as Kangding grass, is a species of perennial grass native to the mountainous regions of China. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues and improve overall well-being. Bioactive constituents of Elymus sclerophyllus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and polysaccharides, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Elymus sclerophyllus is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being incorporated into traditional Chinese medicine formulations, such as soups and tonics.
